items.title: Johor DoE detects air pollution linked to suspected illegal oil dumping at Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG, June 18 — The Johor Department of Environment (DoE) has detected air pollution that was believed to be caused by oil residue following an inspection in Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort here. The DoE team, from the Pasir Gudang branch, were alerted on the matter after worried resident detected a chemical odour since last Saturday. Johor DoE director Mohd Famey Yusoff said the department’s enforcement team and the Taman Pasir Putih police were despatched to the affected area following the complaints.

items.title: PNB nears RM400bil asset milestone amid market headwinds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Permodalan Nasional Bhd's (PNB) assets under management (AUM) stood at RM366.5 billion for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025 (FY25) amid trade tensions, cautious monetary easing and geopolitical volatility. In its Integrated Report 2025, the fund said its AUM brings closer to its LEAP-6 target of RM400 billion by 2027. PNB group chief executive officer and acting president Datuk Rizal Rickman Ramli said PNB's growth in AUM reflected the effectiveness of its customer-centric approach and disciplined investment strategy.

items.title: Half of Malaysians yet to invest, PNB sees room to grow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Half of Malaysians have yet to begin investing, presenting a significant opportunity for Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) as it intensifies efforts to improve financial literacy and encourage long-term wealth creation. PNB group chairman Raja Tan Sri Arshad Raja Tun Uda said a recent nationwide survey showed that despite the widespread adoption of Amanah Saham Nasional Bhd (ASNB) products, about 50 per cent of Malaysians have not started their investment journey. To address the issue, PNB has stepped up financial literacy initiatives targeting Malaysians at different stages of life.

items.title: Johor polls: Over 11,000 cops on duty, 39 hotspots identified
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor police have identified 39 hotspots across the state that will receive increased monitoring during the upcoming 16th Johor state election. He said additional personnel would be deployed to these areas. He said Johor police would mobilise a total force of 11,926 personnel, comprising 610 officers, 11,204 rank-and-file personnel and 112 civil servants.

items.title: Unemployed man pleads guilty to drink-driving charge in Johor
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An unemployed man pleaded guilty at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of drink-driving committed two months ago. Khow Yong Boon, 26, entered the plea before magistrate Arun Noval Dass after the charge was read to him under Section 45A(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Amendment 2020). Khow was detained during an Op Mabuk operation conducted by Batu Pahat district police traffic investigators to detect drink-driving offences.

items.title: Journeys of resilience behind STPM success stories for duo
items.summary: SELAYANG: An Orang Asli student and a visually-impaired candidate were among the 18 national top achievers in the Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) 2025 examination, each overcoming personal challenges to excel academically. Hakimi, from Kampung Paya Mendoi in Kuala Krau, Temerloh, said receiving the award was a moment of gratitude after years of sacrifice by his parents, teachers and himself. As a blind student, said Yeoh, he relied on Braille textbooks and assistive software installed on his laptop to access learning materials.

items.title: Workplace and family-linked cases dominate sexual harassment reports, 388 logged from Jan to May 2026
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, June 18 — A total of 388 sexual harassment cases were recorded during the first five months of this year, according to Deputy Minister of Women, Family and Community Development Lim Hui Ying. She said statistics from the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) also showed a rising trend in reported sexual harassment cases, increasing from 477 cases in 2022 to 1,038 cases last year. “I urge colleagues, employers and family members to provide support to victims.

items.title: Bird flu kills 13,000 seal pups on remote Australian island
items.summary: SYDNEY: A pathogenic strain of bird flu killed more than 13,000 elephant seal pups after infecting a breeding colony on a sub-Antarctic volcanic island, Australian scientists said Thursday. Researchers found the remote Heard and McDonald Islands littered with seal carcasses when they arrived on a research expedition in October 2025. Ground and aerial surveys conducted in October 2025 and January 2026 counted 13,300 dead elephant seal pups.

items.title: Pas' iron-fisted attitude increasingly evident - Tun Faisal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the recent changes in Perikatan Nasional's leadership structure reflect Pas' efforts to consolidate its influence within the coalition. He said the removal of Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali and Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin from the coalition's supreme council has increased Pas' representation on the council to eight members. Tun Faisal described Pas' political manoeuvring as harsh.

items.title: Labuan rescue teams recover missing eight-year-old’s body from Sungai Ganggarak Permai, 50 metres from where slippers dropped
items.summary: LABUAN, June 18 — The body of an eight-year-old boy feared drowned in Sungai Ganggarak Permai here yesterday evening was found today. He was found by search-and-rescue members comprising personnel from rescue agencies and local villagers involved in the search operation. Labuan Fire and Rescue Department director Abdul Rahman Ali said the department received an emergency call at 6.36pm yesterday and immediately launched a search-and-rescue operation.

items.title: No more warnings: Selangor JPJ to haul vehicle owners to court over rentals to foreigners in the state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ) has warned that local vehicle owners found renting out their vehicles to foreigners in the state will no longer be given warnings and will instead be taken straight to court. Selangor JPJ deputy director Datuk Ahmad Kamarunzaman Mehat said motorcycles were among the most commonly rented vehicles, with daily, weekly and monthly arrangements offered at set rates, according to a report published by Kosmo! He said foreigners typically use the rented vehicles for business activities such as selling food, transporting gas cylinders and scrap metal.

items.title: Muda: Good ties with Bersatu in Johor, but no cooperation deal yet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muda has good ties with Bersatu in Johor, but no decision has been made on electoral cooperation ahead of the state election. Its president Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz said Muda had engaged with all opposition parties ahead of the Johor state election but stressed that no conclusive decision had been reached on any electoral arrangement beyond its existing partnership with Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM). Bersatu forges ahead as PN drags feet in Johor, Negri Sembilan polls She said Muda had consistently kept communication channels open with opposition parties, but any cooperation would have to be guided by shared policy priorities.

items.title: Bersatu fails bid to overturn MACC freeze on two bank accounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The High Court has dismissed Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia’s (Bersatu) judicial review application to challenge a freeze order imposed by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) on two of its bank accounts. The Star reported that Justice Aliza Sulaiman, now a Court of Appeal judge, ruled that the applicants failed to prove the MACC’s actions were ultra vires, illegal, procedurally improper, irrational or made in bad faith. Berita Harian in a separate report said that the High Court had earlier on October 30, 2023 granted Bersatu leave to initiate judicial review proceedings against MACC’s seizure of its bank accounts.

items.title: Pasir Gudang 'chemical odour' traced to oil dumped in residential drain
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort residents here were left worried after a mysterious chemical odour hung in their neighbourhood on Saturday. Johor Department of Environment (DoE) director Dr Mohd Famey Yusoff said complaints were received regarding chemical odour pollution in the residential area at 10pm. "Investigations revealed a strong stench resembling that of oil and traces of black oil was found the main drain of the housing estate.

items.title: Political stability crucial to Malaysia's high-income goal
items.summary: Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Sim Tze Tzin said maintaining gross domestic product (GDP) growth and the current economic trajectory would strengthen Malaysia's prospects of crossing the high-income threshold. Sim said external factors, including global trade conditions, economic uncertainties, supply chain disruptions and rising living costs, could affect Malaysia's path towards achieving high-income status. The government must work hard, the private sector must work hard, workers must work hard, and all Malaysians must work hard in terms of productivity and other areas," he said.

items.title: Johor beats national STPM average, 136 score perfect CGPA
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor outperformed the national average in the Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) examination last year, recording a cumulative grade point average (CGPA) of 2.94 compared with the national CGPA of 2.88. Caretaker Johor Education and Information Committee chairman Aznan Tamin congratulated the state's STPM candidates after the results were released today, describing the achievement as a testament to the dedication of students, teachers, parents and the wider education community. In addition, Johor secured two major national accolades, namely the Best STPM 2025 Student in the Science Stream and the National Best Form Six College Award.

items.title: Johor police warn against 3R provocation and disrupting the peace ahead of state election
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — The police will not compromise with any acts that provoke, threaten and incite hatred, including those linked to 3R issue (religion, race and royal institutions), for the upcoming state election. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said this includes acts of damaging public property, spreading false information or committing any offence that could affect public order throughout the election period. Present was Johor deputy police chief Deputy Commissioner Hoo Chuan Huat and senior public order officers.

items.title: India sparred with Telegram days ahead of blocking the app
items.summary: INDIA privately rebuked Telegram for not proactively removing accounts offering purported leaked exam papers while the company accused New Delhi of misrepresenting meeting records, an impasse that led to an unprecedented ban of the app, documents show. Telegram has challenged the ban by Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government in the Delhi High Court. Telegram, which has 150 million users in India, its biggest market, has said in its court papers the ban undermines constitutional protections and free speech rights.

items.title: Johor rolls out economic plan targeting RM167.4b in investments
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — The state government launched the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP) by identifying 83 investment projects across eight priority industries, which are expected to contribute investments worth RM167.4 billion. He said focusing on these industries would strengthen the state’s investment narrative, thereby giving investors confidence in the government’s consistent support. Also present were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, the Economic and Investment Advisor of Johor Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad, and President and Chairman of Pemandu Associates Datuk Seri Idris Jala.

items.title: Removable sunshades, curtains remain illegal on moving vehicles, says RTD
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Public confusion over the use of removable sunshades and curtains on vehicle windows has resurfaced following recent discussions on social media, with many motorists questioning why such accessories are prohibited while certain tinted windows are allowed. Under Malaysian road transport regulations, the use of removable sunshades, curtains or other window coverings while a vehicle is in motion is an offence. Although the regulations mainly govern window tinting, enforcement authorities have also classified curtains, blinds and removable sunshades as prohibited obstructions when a vehicle is being driven.

items.title: Macron calls for global AI rules, warns of risks
items.summary: EVIAN: French President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday called for stronger international regulation of artificial intelligence (AI), warning that its impact on democratic societies can no longer be ignored, German Press Agency (dpa) reported. "But no one can any longer ignore the impact of AI on our democracies and our societies," he said, arguing that regulation was needed at the international level. Macron said several democratic powers would begin building a cooperation platform aimed at establishing common AI standards, including in the field of cybersecurity.

items.title: MMEA seizes cigarettes worth RM212k in Kelantan river operation
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) seized 24 boxes of contraband cigarettes worth about RM212,000 at Kampung Penambang last week. Kelantan MMEA director Maritime Captain Kamal Mohamad said no arrests were made during the 9pm operation on June 13. Kamal said the estimate included the vehicles seized in the operation.

items.title: Indonesia central bank raises rates by 25 basis points
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's central bank raised its benchmark rates by 25 basis points on Thursday, just a week after a surprise off-cycle rate hike, as it seeks to attract fresh capital inflows and halt selling in its battered rupiah and other assets. Bank Indonesia raised the seven-day reverse repurchase rate to 5.75 per cent, the highest level since May 2025. BI also raised the overnight deposit facility rate and lending facility rates by the same amount to 4.75 per cent and 6.50 per cent.

items.title: Wildlife group claims RM53mil elephant transfer payment went to individuals, not govt coffers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A wildlife rights group has alleged that payment from the transfer of three elephants from Malaysia to a zoo in Japan did not go into government coffers, but instead ended up in the hands of some people. Hak Asasi Hidupan Liar Malaysia (Hidup) lawyer Sachpreetraj Singh Sohanpal said the group intended to lodge a complaint with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) over the relocation of three elephants —Dara, Amoi and Kelat—to the Tennoji Zoo in Osaka. "The complaint is on whether the relocation was transparent and if money was involved," he said outside the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya, after the group was denied entry earlier today.

items.title: Carey Island port project on track, transport minister says amid land talks with Selangor govt
items.summary: PORT KLANG, June 18 — The Selangor State Government is finalising the settlement of the land issue as the initial phase of the development of the Port Klang Third Terminal on Carey Island, here. “(The construction of this third port) will involve the Build-Operate-Transfer model. On May 11, Loke was reported to have said that the government had approved the development project of the Third Terminal of Port Klang on Carey Island in Selangor.

items.title: FMM proposes crisis adjustment mechanism under SKDS
items.summary: Its president, Jacob Lee Chor Kok said that under such a mechanism, temporary and targeted quota adjustments could be considered when major external disruptions significantly affect transportation and supply chain operations. Additionally, he said such a mechanism could be implemented through the existing Petroleum Subsidy Approval Committee with an accelerated review process during recognised disruptions. "This would enable genuine operational requirements arising from extraordinary circumstances to be assessed quickly while maintaining the integrity of the subsidy system," Lee said.

items.title: Halim reflects on challenging first month as MACC chief
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman has pledged to drive improvements across the agency, describing his first month at the helm as a challenging but rewarding transition. He acknowledged that leading the anti-graft agency was markedly different from his previous role as a judge, but said public service required those entrusted with responsibility to embrace challenges rather than avoid them. A former High Court judge, Halim assumed office on May 13 after His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, consented to his appointment.

items.title: RM195mil in Bersatu accounts remain frozen after court ruling
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu bank accounts containing RM195 million will remain frozen after the High Court today dismissed the party's challenge against the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's (MACC) freezing and seizure orders. Judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman ruled that Bersatu and its president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin failed to establish that the respondents' decisions were tainted by illegality, irrationality or bad faith. On Oct 30, 2023, the court allowed Bersatu to commence judicial review proceedings to challenge the MACC's decision to freeze its two accounts.

items.title: UNHCR card holder's BMW among 145 luxury vehicles seized by RTD
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A BMW 7 Series car driven by a United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholder was among 145 vehicles impounded by the Road Transport Department (RTD) recently. Negri Sembilan RTD director Jasyindar Singh Sidhu said 870 vehicles were inspected, and 145 vehicles were impounded, including various luxury models such as BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Toyota Alphard, Lexus, and a BMW Limousine. "Throughout the operation, seven luxury vehicles involving non-citizens, primarily from China, Pakistan, and Bangladesh, including a UNHCR cardholder, were impounded," he said in a statement today.

items.title: inDrive records highest monthly ride volume in May with 1.2mil trips
items.summary: Compared with January 2026, completed rides increased 8.7 per cent, while the number of active driver-partners rose 10.2 per cent and passenger numbers grew 1.9 per cent. inDrive Malaysia driver operations manager Azlan Anwar said the platform's growth reflects the contributions of its driver-partners, who play a key role in connecting communities across the country. Azlan said inDrive is among the country's top three e-hailing platforms.

items.title: RMAF eyes next-gen aircraft as Hercules transport fleet reaches 50 years
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA, June 18 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) plans to replace its existing C-130 Hercules transport aircraft in line with growing operational demands and the ageing condition of the fleet. He said rising maintenance costs for ageing aircraft, coupled with the technological advantages and greater payload capacity of newer-generation aircraft, make the replacement of the C-130 a necessity despite the proven capabilities of the current model. According to him, RMAF is also conducting studies on replacing its multi-role combat aircraft and increasing the number of Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) by 2035, as existing assets are expected to face obsolescence and maintenance support issues by then.

items.title: PKFZ snags RM566mil in new investments
items.summary: PORT KLANG: The Port Klang Free Zone (PKFZ) today unveiled RM566 million worth of new investments and launched its Smart Intelligent Industrial Park initiative, marking a major milestone in its transformation into a regional trade, logistics and industrial hub. The projects launched comprise Wisma Golden Horse, the PKFZ Centralised Labour Quarters (CLQ), Suntek Materials Sdn Bhd's factory, Golden Horse Rubber Factory Phase 2 and PKFZ's fully integrated management system under its Smart Intelligent Industrial Park initiative. Loke said PKFZ's transformation into a Smart Intelligent Industrial Park marked a new chapter in Malaysia's digital logistics journey.

items.title: PN restructuring aimed at strengthening coalition, says Tuan Ibrahim
items.summary: PN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the changes, which took immediate effect, were discussed during a meeting yesterday. His remarks came following PN's announcement last night that Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali and Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin had been dropped from the coalition's leadership line-up. PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar had said Azmin had been relieved of his duties as deputy secretary-general, while Radzi was removed as election director.

items.title: Malaysia's trade momentum seen holding through 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade is expected to remain resilient in the coming months, although prospects for maintaining the momentum through the rest of 2026 remain cautiously optimistic, according to the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry. "Malaysia has recorded its highest-ever trade figures, with April marking the strongest trade performance in the country's history. "This shows that Malaysia is resilient.

items.title: South Korea parliament launches probe into election ballot shortages
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea's parliament on Thursday launched a 45-day parliamentary investigation into the National Election Commission after ballot-paper shortages disrupted voting in the June 3 local elections, approving the plan at a plenary session. The special parliamentary committee will examine the NEC and regional election commissions over what lawmakers described as infringements of citizens' voting rights and the need for election-management reform. An NEC official said on Wednesday ballot shortages occurred at 91 polling stations nationwide, with voting briefly suspended at 26 during the local elections.

items.title: Anwar to announce Johor Pakatan state election candidates on Monday
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is expected to announce the list of candidates contesting in the Johor state election this Monday, said Johor PKR chairman Dr Zaliha Mustafa. Zaliha, who is also PKR vice-president, said the announcement was expected to be made at a ceremony involving the party’s top leadership, including PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “The announcement of candidates has been agreed upon by the state and central PH.

items.title: Muda fields 50pct women candidates for Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muda has finalised its list of candidates for the upcoming Johor election, with women making up 50 per cent of those selected, party president Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz said today. She said the party's candidate selection committee had completed the vetting process and finalised the lineup, which would be announced soon. On reports of competing interest from Pakatan Harapan component parties for the Putri Wangsa seat, she said no constituency belonged permanently to any political party.

items.title: New MACC chief Abdul Halim vows to strengthen agency despite steep learning curve
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 18 — Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman has pledged to drive improvements across the agency, acknowledging that his first month in office has presented challenges due to his different professional background. Abdul Halim said every individual entrusted with leadership responsibilities must be prepared to embrace challenges and fulfil their duties without hesitation. On April 25, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, consented to Abdul Halim’s appointment as the new MACC Chief Commissioner.

items.title: UN warns Israeli settlers could join blacklist for violations against children
items.summary: NEW YORK: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres warned on Wednesday that Israeli settler groups could be added to a global blacklist for violations against children as he voiced alarm at a "staggering" rise in violations against Palestinian children. The world body's annual report on Children and Armed Conflict recorded 38,558 "grave violations" globally in 2025, affecting 24,174 children, the latter figure a record since CAAC's mandate began in 1996. The report said 9,465 grave violations were attributed to Israeli forces and 326 to Israeli settlers.

items.title: JETP to drive Johor's RM260b GDP target, balanced growth across districts
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP), launched today, will serve as a major catalyst in realising the Maju Johor 2030 agenda. Caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said development spillovers from JETP would not be confined to the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) but would extend to all 10 districts in the state. "JETP will drive the six core pillars of Maju Johor 2030, namely governance, economic development, social safety nets, basic facilities and needs, safety and sustainability, and youth development.

items.title: RMAF strengthens South China Sea surveillance with drones and new patrol aircraft
items.summary: SUBANG, June 18 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) is progressively strengthening its Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities to enhance monitoring of the country’s maritime zones, particularly in the South China Sea. Chief of Air Force, General Tan Sri Muhamad Norazlan Aris, said the second phase of the initiative will involve procuring three additional ANKA Medium Altitude Long Endurance-Unmanned Aerial System (MALE-UAS) units. Muhamad Norazlan said the RMAF is scheduled to receive its first P-72M maritime patrol aircraft (MPA) from Italy next April, further strengthening air surveillance operations.

items.title: Miri police arrest man over string of five shop break-ins
items.summary: MIRI, June 18 — Police have arrested a 30‑year‑old local man in connection with a string of five break‑ins at business premises in the city. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the district’s Criminal Investigation Division (CID) received several reports of break-ins between mid-May and early June. With the arrest, Mohd Farhan said the district police have resolved all five cases sharing the same modus operandi, reported at Airport Commercial Centre, Assar Commercial Miri Bypass, Mosjaya, Permyjaya and Boulevard Miri, with total losses estimated at RM15,000.

items.title: Palantir wants to 'defend the West,' but the West is wary
items.summary: Palantir was quickly put to use by US troops in Iraq and Afghanistan. According to government tracking site usaspending.gov, Palantir has won more than US$2.7 billion in defence contracts since 2008. Palantir's most contested work involves immigration.

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin: Wawasan RoS status not an issue as party only changed name after takeover
items.summary: Hamzah said the faction he leads had taken over an existing registered party and simply changed its name. Hamzah said MPs present agreed on the need to go to the ground and support candidates as part of the election preparations. Earlier, Hamzah and 16 other MPs from Gerakan Reset Malaysia attended the meeting with PAS MPs, which lasted nearly two hours.

items.title: Grandkids’ birth certificate numbers deliver RM11.8m Toto jackpot to Perak retiree
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — A 62-year-old retired salesman from Perak has won a Sports Toto 4D Jackpot 1 prize worth RM11.8 million after using numbers derived from his grandchildren’s birth certificates. The winner collected RM11,806,469.85 from the total RM12 million jackpot on May 31 after his winning numbers, 1660 and 5093, matched the jackpot combination, according to The Star . The retiree said the numbers were inspired by his two grandchildren’s birth certificate details.

items.title: Johor polls: Muda says relationship with Bersatu positive, but no pact agreed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Muda has maintained “good ties” with Bersatu in Johor, though discussions on a potential electoral pact for the state polls have yet to reach a conclusion. The party’s president, Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, said Muda had begun engagement with all opposition parties on possible cooperation, but no final decision had been made apart from its existing arrangement with Parti Sosialis Malaysia under a “progressive bloc”, according to Free Malaysia Today. Amira, who was formerly Puteri Wangsa assemblyman before the Johor state assembly was dissolved, said any potential partners must align with Muda’s policy direction.

items.title: Penang to unveil financial hub blueprint by August amid push for SME growth
items.summary: They need access to venture capital, private equity, cross-border investment networks and capital market expertise,” he said in his keynote address at the Penang Economic Forum 2026 here today. Chow said the PIFC aims to attract capital inflows, support Penang’s technology ecosystem and provide local businesses with better access to funding and advisory services to innovate, expand internationally and eventually become publicly listed companies. “As at May 17, 2026, Penang had only 106 publicly listed companies.

items.title: Two Malaysian soldiers charged with smuggling three Myanmar migrants through Bukit Kayu Hitam border
items.summary: The accused, Mohd Zulhilmi Aqwa A Rahman, 34, and Mohamad Johan Iskandar Shah Jeffry Pidin, 26, indicated that they understood the charges after they were read separately before Judge Sharmila Abdul Samad, reported Berita Haria n. According to the charge sheet, Mohd Zulhilmi Aqwa was accused of using a white Toyota Yaris to transport two smuggled Myanmar migrants aged 29 and 19. Mohamad Johan Iskandar Shah was separately accused of using a maroon Perodua Bezza to transport a 61-year-old Myanmar migrant who had been smuggled into the country. Mohd Zulhilmi Aqwa was represented by lawyer Datuk Shanmugam Ganesan, while Mohamad Johan Iskandar Shah was unrepresented.

items.title: High Court sets August hearing on MACC bid to block Daim’s widow from managing offshore assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The High Court today fixed August 13 to hear an application by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) seeking to restrain Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid from managing offshore assets valued at US$157.5 million and £85 million located in Jersey, a British Crown territory. Na’imah was exempted from attending today’s proceedings. This marks the third application filed by the prosecution involving overseas assets, following earlier proceedings concerning assets in the United Kingdom and Singapore.

items.title: Batu Pahat man jailed six months after punching wife in face and head over rent arrears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — A delivery worker was sentenced to six months’ jail by the Magistrates’ Court in Batu Pahat today after pleading guilty to punching his wife in the face and head after becoming angry when she asked him to settle their outstanding house rent. Harian Metro reported that Magistrate Arun Noval Dass handed the sentence to Mohamad Norkholis Idris Imam Muryadi, 35, after considering his guilty plea and the facts of the case. Under the first charge, the father of three was accused of intentionally causing hurt to his 32-year-old wife by pushing her against a wall at a store in Parit Raja at about 9am on June 13.

items.title: Terengganu tops STPM rankings for seventh straight year, beats national average
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Terengganu has retained its position as the best-performing state in the country for the Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) examination for the seventh consecutive year after recording a cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of 3.18 for 2025. Terengganu deputy education director Che Wan Zuraidi Che Wan Zaid said the achievement surpassed the national average CGPA of 2.88, Kosmo! Che Wan Zuraidi said 43 of the state’s 46 schools exceeded the national CGPA average, while 36 schools recorded a CGPA of 3.00 or higher.

items.title: STPM 2025: Record 1,336 students score perfect 4.0 CGPA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — A total of 1,336 candidates in the 2025 Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) examination achieved a perfect 4.00 cumulative grade point average (CGPA), up by 70 candidates from 1,266 the previous year. Malaysian Examinations Council (MPM) chairman Professor Datuk Md Amin Md Taff said this year’s national CGPA was also 12.06 per cent higher than the 2.57 recorded in STPM 2013. He said the proportion of candidates obtaining a perfect 4.00 CGPA rose from 3.06 per cent last year to 3.50 per cent this year.

items.title: RMAF to receive first two FA-50M fighter jets in October, says Air Force chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) has confirmed that the first two FA-50M light combat aircraft are scheduled to arrive in Malaysia in October. RMAF chief General Tan Sri Muhamad Norazlan Aris said the remaining aircraft would be delivered in stages through to March 2028, Kosmo! He said this showed that the procurement programme for the 18 aircraft remains on track.

items.title: Perak gardener faces murder charge over roadside killing of contract worker in Kampung Gajah
items.summary: BATU GAJAH, June 18 — A gardener was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a contract worker in Kampung Gajah last week. Mohammad Farizzal Fazly Mohd Zulkifly, 30, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Mohd Harith Mohd Mazlan, who then set July 20 for mention. The man is charged with murdering Muhammad Fahmi Abdullah, 29, on the side of Jalan Utama, Jalan Trans Perak in the Perak Tengah district between 4.30am and 5.49am on June 10 this year.

items.title: Man who attacked motorcyclist with meat cleaver on Federal Highway pleads guilty
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 18 — A salesman pleaded guilty today in the Sessions Court to attacking a motorcyclist with a meat cleaver on the Federal Highway last Thursday. He was charged with intentionally causing grievous hurt to Mohd Fairus Jaafar, 45, using a meat cleaver, which constitutes a deadly weapon, at Section 13 of the Federal Highway along the motorcycle lane at KM14.6 at around 8.19am on June 11. The prosecution, led by Deputy Public Prosecutor Ho Zee Xing, earlier recommended that bail be set at RM30,000 with one surety.

items.title: UMT climbs to highest-ever QS ranking, enters world’s top 47pc universities
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 18 — Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT) has improved its position in the QS World University Rankings (QS WUR) 2027, rising to the 701-710 band globally from 801-850 in the previous year. UMT Vice-Chancellor Dr Mohd Zamri Ibrahim said the achievement places the university among the top 47 per cent of universities worldwide, reflecting its continued progress in advancing academic excellence, research, international collaboration and sustainability on the global stage. “In the QS WUR 2027 assessment, UMT also recorded encouraging performance in the Citations per Faculty (CPF) indicator, achieving a citation index of 159.5 per academic staff member, significantly surpassing the global median of 59.9 and placing UMT among the top seven universities in Malaysia for this indicator,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysia Stadium Corporation CEO charged with RM1.45m graft over Bukit Jalil National Squash Centre tender
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The chief executive officer of Perbadanan Stadium Malaysia (PSM) was charged in the Sessions Court here today with seven counts of corruption involving RM1.45 million in connection with the RM8.7 million tender to upgrade the Bukit Jalil National Squash Centre. According to the first charge, he is accused of soliciting a bribe of RM1 million for himself from Yap Yeow Kuen, 60, at PSM office, Level 2, Stadium Nasional, Kuala Lumpur Sports City (KLSC), Sri Petaling here, on February 24. The money was as a bribe for the accused to agree to choose Aerolux Power Constructions Sdn Bhd, to win the tender for the Upgrading of the Courts & Sports Facilities at the National Squash Centre, Kuala Lumpur Sports City Bukit Jalil here, worth RM8,733,197.

items.title: MAG working with AirBorneo to improve aircraft availability amid operational disruptions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG), through its maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) arm, MAB Engineering Services, remains committed to supporting AirBorneo under its existing maintenance partnership as the Sarawak-owned airline works to overcome recent operational challenges. MAG said MAB Engineering Services continues to provide line, base and component maintenance, as well as warehousing and logistics support to AirBorneo. AirBorneo’s MRO providers include Firefly Maintenance and Repair Organisation and MAB Engineering.

items.title: Johor BN candidate list 80pc complete, says Onn Hafiz, as unselected hopefuls urged to stay loyal
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has urged party members not selected as candidates for the upcoming state election to remain steadfast in the coalition’s struggle. The Johor caretaker menteri besar said efforts to strengthen the party must remain strong, with the people as the primary focus of the struggle. He said this when asked about his advice to individuals who were not selected to contest in the Johor state election this time.

items.title: Onn Hafiz says ‘poster boy’ status does not guarantee Johor MB appointment
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — Being a party’s ‘poster boy’ or principal campaign figure in an election does not guarantee appointment as menteri besar if the party succeeds in forming the state government, Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said. The Johor caretaker menteri besar said royal consent, particularly from the Sultan of Johor Sultan Ibrahim and Regent of Johor Tunku Mahkota Ismail, is crucial in determining who will lead the state administration. Meanwhile, Onn Hafiz shared advice from the Johor royal institution, urging all parties to observe proper conduct and decorum throughout the campaign period.
